title: rhamnus rubra subsp. typica
intro: Rhamnus rubra subsp. typica, also known as Red Alder, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. The medicinal properties of Rhamnus rubra subsp. typica have been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving gastrointestinal issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
